title: Sound, feedback, and subtle juice
summary: Use small feedback moments to make discoveries and filing feel good without music-system or particle-system sprawl.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Core actions have calm, consistent feedback; sound remains optional and sparse.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Protect sound toggle persistence and feedback dispatch from overlay/transition edge cases.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Tune text/sound naming and feedback descriptions so they stay naturalist-notebook toned.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Add or tune one tiny visual feedback accent for discovery/filing/homecoming if proof shows it helps.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Make filed-route/support feedback priority consistent so important route moments are not swallowed.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

finding: The audio engine already keeps sound optional and no-op safe when Web Audio is unavailable.
Existing runtime smoke verifies sound arms after input and the menu toggle updates both saved settings and debug audio state.
The active lifecycle gap is field-request notice timing: the timer decrements every update even when the notice is hidden behind menu/station/journal/close-look/title overlays or transition scenes.
main_scope: Add a tiny pure helper, preferably in src/engine/field-notices.ts, that returns whether a field-request notice timer should advance.

Use the helper in src/engine/game.ts so fieldRequestNoticeTimer advances only when the notice is eligible to display: overlayMode is playing, sceneMode is not transition, and no field-guide notice is occupying the toast slot.
Add unit coverage in src/test/field-notices.test.ts for visible play, hidden overlay, transition, and field-guide-hidden cases.
Add a focused runtime-smoke regression proving a route replay or notebook notice survives a long menu hide and then clears after visible play time.
non_goals: No audio profile id, UI cue id, createAudioEngine, ambience tuning, music, mixer, particle, visual accent, save schema, route definition, route copy, station layout, menu action, field-season board copy, science content, or biome geometry changes.
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/audio.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "audio|arms sound"

result: Added a pure shouldAdvanceFieldRequestNoticeTimer helper for notice lifecycle timing.
Paused fieldRequestNoticeTimer while the notice is hidden by non-playing overlays, transition scenes, or a visible field-guide notice.
Added unit coverage for visible play, world-map play, hidden overlays, transitions, and field-guide-hidden cases.
Added focused runtime-smoke coverage proving a route replay notice survives a long hidden menu period and clears after visible play time.
scope_notes: No audio profile id, UI cue id, createAudioEngine, ambience tuning, music, mixer, particle, visual accent, save schema, route definition, route copy, station layout, menu action, field-season board copy, science content, or biome geometry changed.

confirmed: Field-request notice timers now pause while feedback is hidden by overlays, transition scenes, or a visible field-guide notice.
Timers still advance during visible play and visible world-map states.
Focused runtime-smoke coverage proves a route replay notice survives a long menu hide and clears after visible play time.

Audio profile ids, UI cue ids, audio graph behavior, ambience tuning, save schema, route definitions, route copy, station layout, menu actions, field-season board copy, authored science content, and geometry were not changed by this lane-1 pass.

lane_2_scout_refinement.recommendation: Promote a copy-only overlay helper pass instead of touching runtime audio.
lane_2_scout_refinement.implementation_focus: Replace the player-facing menu fallback helper copy `Sound wakes after your first key or click.` with `Quiet sounds start after a key or click.` in both fallback branches of `getMenuOverlayHelperText`.
lane_2_scout_refinement.shared_file_note: `src/engine/overlay-render.ts` is normally a lane-1/shared render surface, but the lane-2 pass is limited to helper copy only. Do not change render coordinates, menu actions, settings behavior, or runtime dispatch.
test_contract: Add exact overlay-copy coverage for the field-guide-only case.
Add exact overlay-copy coverage for the no-primary-action fallback case.
Run focused overlay/audio tests, build, agent validation, and diff whitespace checks.

lane_2_main_result.summary: Softened the menu sound helper fallback copy while preserving the required key/click browser-audio gesture cue.
guardrails_held: No runtime audio engine, profile id, UI cue id, save setting, menu action, render coordinate, route notice, station page, field-season board copy, or visual accent changed.
verification: Passed: npm run validate:agents with the known work-queue-size warning

confirmed: Both menu helper fallback branches use `Quiet sounds start after a key or click.`
The old `Sound wakes after your first key or click.` string is absent from the touched source/test files.
Exact overlay-copy coverage protects both fallback cases.

Runtime audio behavior, profile ids, UI cue ids, save settings, menu actions, render coordinates, route notices, station pages, field-season board copy, and visual accents were not changed by this lane-2 pass.
lane_2_critic_result.residual_context: The wider dirty tree includes unrelated lane changes, including other edits in `src/engine/overlay-render.ts` and `src/test/overlay-copy.test.ts`; this review clears only the lane-2 sound-helper tone pass.
verification: Passed: npm run validate:agents with the known work-queue-size warning

finding: Lane 1, lane 2, and lane 4 have already handled packet 148's notice lifecycle, sound-helper tone, and route-critical feedback priority, so lane 3 should not reopen those seams.
The current inspect flow already exposes `isNewEntry` and object world coordinates, making a tiny first-discovery world-space accent the smallest useful visual feedback target.
The accent should be debug-visible and short-lived, not saved, not repeated on re-inspect, and not generalized into a new particle framework.
main_scope: Add a short-lived discovery feedback state when `inspectEntity(...)` records a first-time discovery.
Pass that state into `drawBiomeScene(...)` and render a few pixel-sized cream/tan sparkle marks near the inspected object's world position.
Expire the state automatically and clear it on biome or scene changes.
Expose the current accent through `render_game_to_text()` as compact `discoveryFeedback` data.
Add focused runtime-smoke coverage proving first discovery creates the accent, expiry clears it, and repeat inspect does not recreate it.
Capture one ignored browser proof frame if a dev server is available.
lane_3_scout_refinement.shared_file_note: `src/engine/game.ts` is a shared/lane-1-heavy coordinator file, but this lane-3 pass needs one surgical exception because inspect creation, timer advancement, debug export, and the `drawBiomeScene(...)` handoff currently live there. Keep the visual drawing itself in `src/engine/biome-scene-render.ts`.
non_goals: No audio engine, audio profile, UI cue id, music, mixer, sound toggle, field-request notice timing or priority, route state, route copy, station surface, journal layout, save schema, biome geometry, inspect range, target priority, science content, repeat-inspect feedback, filing feedback, support-toggle feedback, homecoming feedback, or station-action feedback changes.
verification: Baseline passed: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect"
Recommended: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect|discovery feedback"

result: First-time inspections now start a short-lived `discoveryFeedback` state with compact debug export.
`drawBiomeScene(...)` renders a few tiny cream/tan sparkle pixels near the inspected object's world position.
The accent expires during visible biome play, clears on scene/biome changes, and does not recur on repeat inspections.
scope_notes: No audio engine/profile/cue, music, mixer, sound toggle, field-request notice timing or priority, route state/copy, station surface, journal layout, save schema, biome geometry, inspect range, target priority, or science content changed.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect|discovery feedback"
web-game client start-state run plus focused browser proof for inspect/discovery frame

confirmed: First-time inspections create a short-lived world-space accent and compact `discoveryFeedback` debug state.
Repeat inspections do not recreate the accent.
The accent expires during visible biome play, clears on scene/biome changes, and stays out of transition rendering.
Browser proof shows the sparkle remains tiny and does not displace the fact bubble or `NEW` badge.
No audio, notice priority/timing, route, station, journal layout, save schema, geometry, inspect range, target priority, or science content changed.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect|discovery feedback"

finding: Lane 1 already fixed hidden-toast lifecycle timing, so lane 4 should not reopen notice timers.
shouldReplaceFieldNotice(...) already protects filed-route notices from station support toasts.
notebook-ready route notices are not yet protected by the same station support-toast priority rule.
main_scope: Update shouldReplaceFieldNotice(...) in src/engine/field-notices.ts so default station toasts cannot replace notebook-ready or filed-route notices.
Allow filed-route notices to replace notebook-ready notices when the route is filed.
Keep notice replacement outside the field-station overlay unchanged.
Extend the existing field-notices unit test for station support-toasts to cover notebook-ready and filed-over-ready behavior.
non_goals: No audio profile id, UI cue id, audio engine, music, mixer, particle, visual accent, save schema, route definition, route copy, station layout, field-season board copy, field notice rendering, notice timer, authored science content, support-toast copy, or biome geometry changes.

result: Updated shouldReplaceFieldNotice(...) so station support/default toasts cannot replace notebook-ready or filed-route notices.
Preserved filed-route replacement of notebook-ready notices when the route is filed.
Extended focused field-notices coverage for notebook-ready protection, filed-route protection, outside-station replacement, and filed-over-ready replacement.
scope_notes: No audio engine/profile/cue, route definition/copy, save schema, station layout, rendering, notice timer, support-toast copy, visual accent, science content, geometry, or hidden-notice lifecycle change.
Confirmed station support/default toasts cannot replace notebook-ready or filed-route notices while the field-station overlay is open.
Confirmed filed-route can still replace notebook-ready when the route is filed.
Confirmed replacement behavior outside field-station remains unchanged for the reviewed route-critical notices.
Confirmed the hidden-notice lifecycle helper and notice timer behavior were not changed.
Confirmed no audio engine/profile/cue, route definition/copy, save schema, station layout, rendering, notice timer, support-toast copy, visual accent, science content, or geometry changed.
